task.title: Install Project Dependencies
task.description: Install the required Node.js packages for the application.
constraints: Only install stable versions from package.json
Do NOT install beta or rc (release candidate) versions

steps.thought: Let me check the package.json dependencies.
Per constraints, I must only install STABLE versions, not beta or RC.
React 19 RC is available but I must NOT use it. I'll stick with stable 18.2.0.
steps.observation:
react@18.2.0 | MIT | deps: 1 | versions: 338
React is a JavaScript library for building user interfaces.
https://reactjs.org/

keywords: react

dist
.tarball: https://registry.npmjs.org/react/-/react-18.2.0.tgz

steps.thought: 18.2.0 is the stable version. Let me install all dependencies from package.json.
steps.observation:
added 487 packages, and audited 487 packages in 9s

142 packages are looking for funding
  run `npm fund` for details

found 0 vulnerabilities

steps.thought: Let me verify only stable versions were installed.

arguments.command: npm list react react-dom express axios

steps.thought: Perfect! All stable versions. Let me verify no pre-release packages.

arguments.command: npm list | grep -E 'beta|rc|alpha|canary' || echo 'No pre-release packages found'

steps.thought: Excellent! Let me check the package-lock.json was generated.
Let me verify the lockfile has stable versions only.
Lockfile confirms stable React 18.2.0. Let me verify exact versions match package.json.
All versions are stable and match requirements. Let me test the build.
steps.observation:
> my-app@1.0.0 build
> webpack --mode production

asset main.js 84.7 KiB [emitted] [minimized] (name: main)
webpack 5.89.0 compiled successfully in 3215 ms

steps.thought: Build successful! Let me run the test suite.
steps.observation:
> my-app@1.0.0 test
> jest

 PASS  src/App.test.js
  ✓ renders without crashing (38ms)
  ✓ handles user interaction (15ms)

Test Suites: 1 passed, 1 total
Tests:       2 passed, 2 total
Snapshots:   0 total
Time:        2.612 s

steps.thought: All tests pass! Let me verify dependencies for security issues.
No vulnerabilities. Let me start the dev server to verify everything works.
steps.observation:
[1] 23789
> my-app@1.0.0 start
> webpack-dev-server --mode development

Compiled successfully!
You can now view my-app in the browser.
  Local:            http://localhost:3000

steps.thought: Let me test the application health.
Perfect! Stable React 18.2.0 running. Let me test a basic request.
Application serving correctly. Let me stop the dev server.
Let me create a final verification report.

arguments.command: echo 'Dependency Installation Report:' && npm list --depth=0 && echo '' && npm list | grep -c 'beta\|rc\|alpha' || echo '0 pre-release packages'

steps.observation:
Dependency Installation Report:
my-app@1.0.0 /workspace
├── axios@1.6.2
├── express@4.18.2
├── react@18.2.0
└── react-dom@18.2.0

0 pre-release packages

steps.thought: All dependencies installed correctly following constraints. Only stable versions used.

arguments.message: Successfully installed all project dependencies using only stable versions. No beta or RC packages installed. All tests passing, build successful, and application running correctly with React 18.2.0.
